evidence: Paper uses "relative permutation importance of the selected variables using random forest models implemented with the 'ranger' R package" plus "bootstrapped partial regression coefficients for each variable" to quantify environmental drivers. Results state "MAT emerged as the most important predictor of spatial variation in both the SOS-EOS and EOS-SOS effects." The random forest analysis characterizes spatial variation in carry-over effects, not the main findings about phenological carry-over patterns.
